value: Layer 4 cost model outputs by condition with year-based projections from Layer 3.

Ontario-level condition cost model; not city-level allocation yet.
In 2024, the model estimates about $101,001,890 in total costs across the tracked conditions. About $32,452,471 (32.1%) is classified as potentially avoidable.
source_or_notes: Computed from layer4_cost_analysis.csv totals by year.

value: In 2034, the model estimates about $125,128,551 in total costs across the tracked conditions. About $40,054,172 (32.0%) is classified as potentially avoidable.
source_or_notes: Computed from layer4_cost_analysis.csv totals by year.

value: From 2024 to 2034, projected avoidable cost grows from about $32,452,471 to $40,054,172 (about 23.4% growth).
source_or_notes: Computed from yearly avoidable cost totals.

value: The largest avoidable cost drivers in 2034 are: Mental Health ($10,579,491), COPD ($6,917,065), Chronic Kidney Disease ($6,050,870).
Total yearly cost per condition is admissions multiplied by average cost per admission.
source_or_notes: Admissions come from Layer 3 trajectory (Historical for 2024, Reference for future years).

value: Avoidable yearly cost comes from projected avoidable admissions multiplied by average cost per admission.
source_or_notes: Avoidable admissions are sourced from layer3_predictive_trajectory.csv.

value: The avoidable percentage shows what share of total cost could potentially be prevented.
Portfolio share tells how much each condition contributes to the total cost or avoidable cost pool in a year.
formula_or_definition: condition_share_of_layer4_total_cost_YEAR_pct = (condition total_cost_YEAR / sum of all condition total_cost_YEAR) * 100
source_or_notes: Same logic applied for avoidable cost share columns.

value: Baseline year uses historical values; projected years use the reference growth trajectory.
source_or_notes: Selection rule used in transformation pipeline.
